intro: Knema stenophylla, also known as the Narrow-leaved Knema, is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Knema stenophylla include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
